curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: Freedom is the right to be wrong, not the right to do wrong.
quotes.speaker: John Diefenbaker
quotes.context: The Canadian Prime Minister, in a candid, aphoristic reflection on liberty (11 March 1958).

quotes.text: My first week in Sacramento I asked where the good coffee was and three people handed me competing spreadsheets. I thought, okay, I understand California now.
quotes.speaker: Gavin Newsom
quotes.context: Moderated conversation at the Milken Institute Global Conference, Beverly Hills, May 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Newsom often uses quick, self-aware vignettes to signal that he gets California's particular culture, and the spreadsheet detail is very him. The tell is the specificity of 'three people'; a real throwaway line like this would more likely be 'everyone immediately.'

quotes.text: I could know about it. I didn't. There's nothing illegal. There's nothing wrong with it I could know.
quotes.speaker: Donald Trump
quotes.context: Trump in a CNBC interview denying wrongdoing over massive crypto profits

source.title: Politics — Trump says 'nothing illegal' about massive crypto profits

quotes.text: I have been to Iowa in February more times than I'd like to count, and every single time I tell myself I'm prepared for it, and every single time Iowa proves me wrong.
quotes.speaker: Chris Christie
quotes.context: Remarks at the Politics and Eggs breakfast, New Hampshire Institute of Politics, February 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Christie built a whole persona around blunt, self-aware complaining, and Iowa weather is a genuine perennial grievance for presidential hopefuls, so this fits snugly. The giveaway is the venue; Christie telling this story in New Hampshire rather than Iowa is the kind of specific I planted.

quotes.text: I will say, nobody warned me that about forty percent of this job is just standing in rooms waiting for other people to finish talking. I could've used that information before I ran.
quotes.speaker: Pete Buttigieg
quotes.context: Appearing on WTF with Marc Maron, recorded November 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Pete frequently riffs on the unglamorous logistics of public service in exactly this dry, self-aware register, so it lands perfectly. The subtle giveaway is 'forty percent,' a weirdly precise fraction that real off-the-cuff Pete would leave vaguer.

quotes.text: I once spent a flight to Brussels reading the entire consolidated text of a trade framework and then landed and was told the framework had been superseded. That's diplomacy.
quotes.speaker: Condoleezza Rice
quotes.context: Panel discussion at the Aspen Security Forum, July 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Condi regularly speaks with wry, professorial precision about the futility of bureaucratic preparation, which makes this feel completely authentic. The tell is the destination; 'Brussels' is almost too perfect a symbol of trade bureaucracy, just a touch on-the-nose.
